title: AC Series RLC Circuit
description: A fundamental AC circuit schematic representing the series connection of an alternating voltage source, a resistor, an inductor, and a capacitor, complete with phase formulas and resonance parameters.
theory: An AC Series RLC Circuit contains a resistor of resistance R, an inductor of inductance L, and a capacitor of capacitance C connected in series across an alternating voltage source. The current (I) is identical at all points in the circuit. The voltage drops across the components are: V_R = I * R (in phase with current), V_L = I * X_L (leads current by 90 degrees), and V_C = I * X_C (lags current by 90 degrees). The net reactive voltage is (V_L - V_C), which leads or lags the current depending on whether X_L > X_C or X_C > X_L. The total impedance is Z = R + j(X_L - X_C) = sqrt(R^2 + (X_L - X_C)^2) with phase angle θ = arctan((X_L - X_C)/R). At the resonant frequency f_r = 1 / (2π√LC), the inductive and capacitive reactances cancel out (X_L = X_C), minimizing impedance to Z = R, maximizing current, and forcing a unity power factor.
keyPoints: Series Connection: The same alternating current i(t) flows through R, L, and C.
Resistor Voltage: V_R is in phase with the current i(t).
Inductor Voltage: V_L leads the current i(t) by 90 degrees.
Capacitor Voltage: V_C lags the current i(t) by 90 degrees.
Impedance: Z = sqrt(R^2 + (X_L - X_C)^2) where X_L = 2πfL and X_C = 1/(2πfC).
Resonance: Occurs when X_L = X_C, resulting in minimum impedance Z = R and maximum current.
Power Factor: cos(θ) = R / Z. Lagging for inductive circuits, leading for capacitive circuits, and unity at resonance.
examTip: In a series RLC circuit, resonance occurs when X_L = X_C. At resonance: Impedance is minimum (Z = R), Current is maximum, Power factor is unity (cos(θ) = 1), and Resonant frequency is f_r = 1 / (2π√LC). Remember that V_L and V_C are 180° out of phase, so they oppose each other directly.
useCases: Tuning circuits in radio receivers and television tuners to select desired stations.
Filter networks (bandpass, bandstop, highpass, lowpass) in signal processing.
Impedance matching networks to maximize power transfer in RF transmitters.
Induction heating and wireless power transfer systems.
Transient protection and snubber networks in power electronics.

wires.comment: AC source top terminal to top-left corner
Top rail to resistor left lead
Resistor right lead to inductor left lead
Inductor right lead to capacitor left lead
Capacitor right lead to top-right corner
Right rail down to bottom-right corner
AC source bottom terminal to bottom-left corner
